alternatives.when: repeat file reads dominate a session and its SHA-256 dedup cache is worth the extra moving parts (an identical re-read collapses to a roughly 13-token reference)
you want rule-based, no-LLM distillation of command output with the raw text still retrievable on demand, plus a native MCP server

recommendations.rationale: For cutting the token cost of shell command output, rtk is the default: it is the most established of the three, holds a best verdict, and runs as a transparent proxy over common dev commands. sqz is effectively an rtk superset when repeat reads dominate, since its dedup cache turns an identical re-read into a short reference token. omni fits agents that prefer rule-based distillers with retrievable raw output and a native MCP server. All three keep their core mechanism free of any model dependency, so none adds an LLM call to the hot path.

alternatives.when: you want codegraph's actively developed Rust successor with much broader scope (80+ MCP tools, 50+ languages, multi-branch indexing) and can accept a far smaller community
you need zero-server, in-browser code intelligence or a global multi-repo registry, run on macOS or Linux (Windows has stability bugs), and can accept the PolyForm NC licence for commercial use
you want a single native binary with Cypher queries over a SQLite graph and the widest language coverage

recommendations.rationale: For an AST knowledge-graph over the codebase, codegraph is the default: it is MIT-licensed, the most battle-tested by a wide margin, and the reference implementation the others measure themselves against. tokensave is its actively developed Rust successor with much broader scope, worth choosing when its extra tooling matters and a small community is acceptable. gitnexus suits zero-server, in-browser use and multi-repo registries, tempered by Windows stability bugs and a non-free commercial tier. codebase-memory-mcp is a single native binary with Cypher queries and the widest language coverage. All four carry confirmed source-code evidence.
